sglang.title: Optimize triton attention custom mask
sglang.author: ispobock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Skip custom mask for prefix part of triton attention to accelerate target verify stage.
python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf  --speculative-algo EAGLE --speculative-draft lmzheng/sglang-EAGLE-llama2-chat-7B --speculative-num-steps 2 --speculative-eagle-top

sglang.title: [Bugfix] Fix scores mask for moe topk
sglang.author: chenxb002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
In the implementation of grouped topk in MoE layer, the scores of masked groups are set to 0, which may leads to select incorrect experts in certain scenarios.
Modifications
In SGLang, the mask scores are set to 0. This configuration may result in the selection of experts in masked groups

sglang.title: [Docs] Modify ep related server args and remove cublas part of deepseek
sglang.author: Fridge003
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

There are issues like #3704 #3537 that get confused with enable-ep-moe and  ep-size arguments. This PR helps to clarify these arguments.
Also, we figured out cublas feature cannot be applied to SGLang effectively, so this part of doc should be removed.
Modifications


Put enable-ep-moe a

vllm.title: [ci] Fix spec decode test
vllm.author: khluu
vllm.bodyExcerpt: since JackFram/llama-160m is in the list of S3 models, the load format is switched to runai streamer and JackFram/llama-68m also gets loaded with runai streamer as a result. However, runai streamer only works with safetensors

vllm.title: [2/n][ci] S3: Use full model path
vllm.author: khluu
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Use s3://..../<account>/<model_name> instead of s3://.../<model_name>

vllm.title: [Kernel] LoRA - Refactor sgmv kernels
vllm.author: varun-sundar-rabindranath
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Refactor / Modularize sgmv_shrink and sgmv_expand kernels to enable better code re-use.

vllm.title: Merge similar examples in `offline_inference` into single `basic` example
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation:

Reduces number of unnecessary examples reducing clutter
Basic examples are now fully configurable, making them more useful:

for experimentation for new users
as debugging tools for developers



Changes:

Move basic.py, chat.py, classify.py, embed.py, generate.py, score.py to examples/

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix deepseekv3 grouped topk error
vllm.author: chenxb002
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix the logic in grouped topk computation of fused moe.
There is a slight difference between vllm grouped_topk and the official code. When the newly-introduced bias term (e_score_correction_bias in vllm) is not None, we should firstly get the top-2 scores of each group, and use the summation to get

vllm.title: Update `pre-commit`'s `isort` version to remove warnings
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Removes:
[WARNING] repo `https://github.com/PyCQA/isort` uses deprecated stage names (commit, merge-commit, push) which will be removed in a future version.  Hint: often `pre-commit autoupdate --repo https://github.com/PyCQA/isort` will fix this.  if it does not -- consider reporting an issue to tha

vllm.title: [V1][Minor] Print KV cache size in token counts
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Change

from:

INFO 02-20 00:01:02 kv_cache_utils.py:522] # GPU blocks: 127626
INFO 02-20 00:01:02 kv_cache_utils.py:525] Maximum concurrency for 2048 tokens per request: 997.08x


to:

INFO 02-19 23:56:55 kv_cache_utils.py:524] GPU KV cache size: 2,042,016 tokens
INFO 02-19 23:56:55 kv_cache_utils.

vllm.title: fix neuron performance issue
vllm.author: ajayvohra2005
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This fixes a Neuron specific performance issue. Without this fix, Neuron performance degrades quickly when number of concurrent requests >= max_num_seqs.

vllm.title: [Frontend] Add backend-specific options for guided decoding
vllm.author: joerunde
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR extends the guided decoding backend name to support a list of backend-specific options. These are specified in a comma separated list after a colon. This allows us to easily add support for backend-specific features, like xgrammar:json-any-whitespace.
Specifically this PR also implements the

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix max_num_batched_tokens for MLA
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Without this change we are stuck with a very short allowed prompt length by default with deepseek models
Before this PR:
vllm serve /home/vllm-dev/DeepSeek-R1 --trust-remote-code --tensor-parallel-size 8
...
WARNING 02-20 17:08:38 scheduler.py:949] Input prompt (100005 tokens) is too long and exceed

vllm.title: [Neuron][Kernel] Vectorize KV cache load in FlashPagedAttention to maximize DMA bandwidth
vllm.author: lingfanyu

vllm.title: Add llmaz as another integration
vllm.author: kerthcet
vllm.bodyExcerpt: llmaz is an inference platform building on top of kubernetes, it uses vllm as the default inference backend, would like to introduce it as one integration of vllm project.

vllm.title: [Misc] Adding script to setup ray for multi-node vllm deployments
vllm.author: Edwinhr716
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #8302 (link existing issues this PR will resolve)
This script only starts the ray workers, and stalls until all ray workers have been started. It doesn't start up the vllm server itself. The expected command would be
/vllm-workspace/examples/online-serving/multi-node-serving.sh leader --ray_clus

vllm.title: [NVIDIA] Fix an issue to use current stream for the nvfp4 quant
vllm.author: kaixih
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This uses the current stream (as a common practice for other cuda ops).

vllm.title: Use pre-commit to update `requirements-test.txt`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Automatically runs pip compile using pre-commit.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Add `mm_processor_kwargs` to chat-related protocols
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt: #13533 added a new mm_processor_kwarg field to ChatCompletionRequest but it should be added to all chat-related protocols with messages: List[ChatCompletionMessageParam]. This PR fixes this issue.

vllm.title: [V1][Sampler] Avoid an operation during temperature application
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: We can just set greedy entries to -1.0 and then there's no need to replace those entries when we sample.

vllm.title: Missing comment explaining VDR variable in GGUF kernels
vllm.author: SzymonOzog
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Taken from https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/3d68f034dad53f0f27ad626b2732ef48fbcea4ee/ggml/src/ggml-cuda/vecdotq.cuh#L18

vllm.title: [FEATURE] Enables /score endpoint for embedding models
vllm.author: gmarinho2
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Enables /score endpoint for all embedding models via API.
Issue: [FEATURE] Enables offline /score for embedding models (2/2)

vllm.title: [Kernel]Add streamK for block-quantized CUTLASS kernels
vllm.author: Hongbosherlock
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Thanks to #11868
Currently only supports scale_a block shapes of 1x128 and scale_b block shapes of 128x128 (for deepseek v3)
This CUTLASS GEMM is slower when K is very large, such as m128-n1536-k7168 in deepseek v3.
To address this, I explored using both Stream-K and Split-K strategies to accelerate

vllm.title: [Bugfix][CPU] Fix cpu all-reduce using native pytorch implementation
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Different from ipex.distributed, all_reduce implementation from torch.distributed won't return all-reduced tensor:
https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/0b0da81021e061c021e515bc35d7dc0dbbb05941/torch/distributed/distributed_c10d.py#L2753-L2755
